content.text:
Diff was empty — checking staged changes and whether a JS/CSS issue is blocking the tab handlers.

[REDACTED]

input.command: cd /Users/amin/dev/p-projects/armenia/resume && git status --short relocation_jobs/static/ && git diff HEAD -- relocation_jobs/static/js/apply.js relocation_jobs/static/apply.html | head -500
input.description: Check apply file status and HEAD diff

content.text:
The tab logic toggles a `hidden` class — checking whether that CSS class actually exists and applies here.

[REDACTED]

Root cause: tabs toggle a `hidden` class, but CSS only hides the `[hidden]` attribute — so panel visibility never changes. Fixing that to match the rest of the UI.

[REDACTED]

Confirmed: panels use `class="hidden"`, but this page only styles the `[hidden]` attribute. Switching tabs will use the attribute to match the rest of the apply UI.

[REDACTED]

Fixed. The tab buttons were updating the active style, but panels never actually switched.

**Cause:** `setTab` toggled a `hidden` CSS class, but this page only hides elements via the `[hidden]` attribute (see `.apply-layout [hidden]`). There is no general `.hidden { display: none }` rule, so Master resumes / Projects stayed visible (and stacked).

**Change:** use the `hidden` attribute for panel and content visibility (same pattern as the rest of the apply UI), and bump the script cache to `?v=10`. Soft refresh the apply page and the tabs should switch cleanly.

[REDACTED]
